rules: Use the TypeScript API instead of hand-writing raw Excalidraw element dictionaries.
Prefer layout helpers for structure and connections.
Embed bundled SVG assets when an asset alias is listed.
Return saved artifact paths after generation.

review_rubric.pass: Readable diagram, relevant assets, requested structure visible, valid Excalidraw JSON, rendered PNG exists.
review_rubric.needs_review: Structurally close but crowded, weakly labeled, or missing a secondary branch.
review_rubric.fail: Missing or invalid artifacts, blank PNG, wrong asset pack, or requested system shape is not visible.

scenarios.title: Basic Service Flow
scenarios.prompt: Use excalidraw-diagrams to draw a clear left-to-right service flow. A request enters an API connector, moves to an agent worker, passes a guardrail check, and stores the accepted event in a historical database. Add a short title and one short label per arrow. Save the Excalidraw JSON and PNG under examples/out/agent-evals/basic-service-flow.

required_structure: Four primary nodes in the requested order.
At least three directional arrows.
One short title.
Short labels on arrows.
manual_review_focus: Labels are readable in the PNG.
The accepted event lands in the database.

scenarios.title: RAG Answer Trace
scenarios.prompt: Use excalidraw-diagrams to draw a RAG answer trace for a support assistant. Show the user prompt, prompt template, retriever, vector database, LLM answer, audit log, and optional human review. The retrieval path should loop through the vector database before the LLM answers. The audit path should branch from the LLM answer. Save the Excalidraw JSON and PNG under examples/out/agent-evals/rag-answer-trace.

expected_output.layout: main answer path with side audit branch
required_structure: Prompt/template stage appears before retrieval.
Retriever connects to vector database and then to the LLM answer.
Audit log branches from the LLM answer.
Human review is optional or conditional, not the main path.
manual_review_focus: The diagram can be understood without reading the source script.

scenarios.title: Trading Risk Gate
scenarios.prompt: Use excalidraw-diagrams with the trading asset pack to draw a trading risk gate. Market candles produce a target signal, the signal enters a locked risk gate, and only approved trades continue through position sizing to the order book. Rejected trades should branch to a stop-loss or blocked-trade outcome. Label the pass and reject branches. Save the Excalidraw JSON and PNG under examples/out/agent-evals/trading-risk-gate.

expected_output.layout: main approved path plus rejected branch
required_structure: Market candles feed a target signal.
Target signal enters a locked risk gate.
Approved branch continues to position sizing and order book.
Rejected branch ends at stop-loss or blocked-trade outcome.
Pass and reject branches are labeled.
manual_review_focus: Trading asset pack is actually used.
The risk gate is the decision point.

scenarios.title: Model Training Feedback Loop
scenarios.prompt: Use excalidraw-diagrams to draw an ML training and monitoring feedback loop. Data starts in a data lake, moves through feature engineering, train/test split, model training, model validation, model registry, deployment, and monitoring. If drift is detected, the monitoring stage should loop back to model refresh or training. Save the Excalidraw JSON and PNG under examples/out/agent-evals/model-training-feedback-loop.

required_structure: Main training path is ordered and directional.
Validation appears before registry/deployment.
Monitoring appears after deployment.
Drift detection loops back to refresh or training.
manual_review_focus: The feedback arrow is visible and not confused with the main path.
The diagram remains readable despite many nodes.
Stage labels are short enough to fit.

scenarios.title: Agent Evaluation Harness
scenarios.prompt: Use excalidraw-diagrams to draw the harness that runs these diagram scenarios. Show a scenario prompt, an agent planner, a sandbox executor, a generated Excalidraw JSON artifact, a PNG renderer, a PNG artifact, and a human review step. Include labels for the two saved files. Save the Excalidraw JSON and PNG under examples/out/agent-evals/agent-evaluation-harness.

expected_output.layout: artifact chain from prompt to review
required_structure: Scenario prompt feeds an agent planner.
Planner invokes a sandbox executor.
Executor writes a .excalidraw artifact.
Renderer produces a PNG artifact.
Human review consumes the PNG.
Both artifact paths are visible as labels.
manual_review_focus: The renderer step is distinguishable from the JSON artifact.
The diagram explains how this eval set is meant to run.
